articles.title: Tesla blocks stockholders with less than 3% shares from suing officers on its behalf
articles.summary: Tesla has amended its bylaws to prevent shareholders owning less than 3% of its shares from filing derivative lawsuits against its directors or officers. This move, enabled by a new Texas law, raises the threshold significantly, as 3% of Tesla's shares are worth approximately $34 billion. The change follows a Delaware judge's invalidation of CEO Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package, which was challenged by a shareholder owning only nine shares.

articles.title: Tesla adds longtime Chipotle executive Jack Hartung to board of directors
articles.summary: Tesla has appointed Jack Hartung, a long-time Chipotle executive, to its board of directors, effective June 1. Hartung, known for his financial discipline at Chipotle, will join Tesla's audit committee amidst declining EV sales and profits, and concerns about CEO Elon Musk's divided attention. This appointment will expand Tesla's board to nine members.

articles.title: Whitestone REIT acquires 31,832-square-foot retail center in Austin
articles.summary: Whitestone REIT has acquired the 31,832-square-foot San Clemente at Davenport retail center in Austin, Texas. This acquisition is strategically located near Apple and Tesla campuses and complements Whitestone's existing Davenport Village retail center, strengthening its presence in the Austin market. Whitestone plans to enhance the property's performance and long-term growth by leveraging its market knowledge and retailer relationships.

articles.title: What Lessons Can Investors Learn From Cathie Wood's Ark Invest's Aggressive Investment Philosophy?
articles.summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest focuses on aggressive investments in cutting-edge technologies like blockchain, AI, and robotics, aiming for high rewards despite high risks. While Ark has had notable successes with early investments in Bitcoin, Tesla, and Nvidia, its flagship Ark Innovation ETF has seen significant downturns and negative returns more recently. The article suggests that while innovation-focused investing can yield huge gains, implementing an innovation-only portfolio carries substantial risk, emphasizing the difficulty of consistently picking winning companies.

articles.title: GM names Aurora co-founder Sterling Anderson as chief product officer
articles.summary: General Motors has appointed Sterling Anderson, co-founder and chief product officer of Aurora, as its executive VP, global product, and chief product officer. Anderson will oversee the entire product lifecycle for GM's gas and electric vehicles, focusing on tighter integration between software and hardware and emphasizing customer experience. His expertise in automotive engineering and software innovation, honed at Aurora and Tesla, is expected to accelerate GM's product transformation during a critical industry period.
